message:
[P2] fcp-store: pin put-quota TOCTOU fix with concurrent-insert regression test

Re-closes flywheel_connectors-sfunf.

The bead described a TOCTOU between the `used_bytes.load()` inside
`MemoryObjectStore::put`'s `objects.write()` critical section and the
deferred `used_bytes.fetch_add()` after `drop(objects)`. The fix
itself (move `fetch_add` inside the critical section, ahead of the
drop) was already landed at object_store.rs:430 when I arrived on
this bead — but the repo had no concurrency regression test to pin
the ordering and prevent a future refactor from re-opening the
window. The pre-close comment thread recommended exactly such a
stress test as the verification step.

This commit adds `put_quota_holds_under_concurrent_inserts` which:

- Configures `max_bytes = 5_000` with body length 500 (total cost
  1012 per object including the 512-byte header estimate from
  `object_size`); the quota admits at most 4 concurrent inserts.
- Spawns 16 `put` tasks via `fcp_async_core::task::spawn`, each with
  a distinct `object_id` so the duplicate-key short-circuit never
  masks the quota path.
- Joins every task and counts accepted vs. quota-rejected outcomes,
  panicking on any unexpected error variant.
- Asserts `storage_used() <= max_bytes` after all tasks resolve —
  regardless of scheduling, the final state must never exceed the
  configured quota.

Against the pre-fix code this test would have failed
non-deterministically under the interleaving documented in the bead
(three tasks observing pre-commit `used=0`, all passing their quota
check, all committing for a final `used > max_bytes`). Against the
current code it passes every run because the `fetch_add` is now
serialized by the same `objects.write()` guard that covers the quota
read.

Verification:
  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-p7-t6 cargo test -p fcp-store --lib
    → 787 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored.
  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-p7-t6 cargo test -p fcp-store --lib put_quota_holds_under_concurrent_inserts
    → 1 passed; 0 failed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
